title: Redirect Semantics, Method Rewriting, Credential Scope, and Loop Control · Browser and Node Clien… · Contract Lens: Manual Redirect Defense
summary: A sign-in flow follows relative and cross-origin redirects while Fetch redirect modes, credentials, methods, and authorization scope differ. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts manual redirect defense increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch auditable redirect policy engine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose automatic, manual, or rejected redirects from method replay, SSRF, credential, audit, and user-consent risk. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Strip or deliberately reissue authorization, cookies, origin-sensitive fields, and bodies when authority changes. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
